headline: Sony TV Casting Kingmaker Dawn Steinberg Drops Mic After Two Decades of Star-Making
lede: In a seismic shift for television talent acquisition, Sony Pictures Television's longtime head of casting Dawn Steinberg is stepping away after nearly a quarter-century of industry-defining talent discovery.
body:
Dawn Steinberg's departure from Sony Pictures Television represents more than a routine executive transition — it marks the end of an era in television casting that shaped multiple generations of on-screen talent.

Over her 23-year tenure, Steinberg assembled dream teams for some of the most critically acclaimed series of the modern television renaissance. Her remarkable eye for talent powered breakthrough casting in landmark shows like *Breaking Bad*, where she helped discover Bryan Cranston's transformative Walter White, and *Outlander*, which launched international stars Sam Heughan and Caitriona Balfe.

Industry insiders describe Steinberg as a casting alchemist who consistently transformed promising actors into global celebrities. Her collaborative approach with showrunners and production teams made her a behind-the-scenes legend, consistently identifying performers who could elevate complex narrative material.

While Sony has not yet announced Steinberg's immediate successor, her exit creates a significant leadership vacuum in the television casting ecosystem. Her strategic approach to talent development set industry benchmarks that will likely influence casting strategies for years to come.

Steinberg's career trajectory represents a masterclass in entertainment talent strategy, having guided Sony's casting vision through multiple technological and cultural shifts in the entertainment landscape.
key_facts: Shaped multiple generations of on-screen talent
